text:
You are building an admin product catalog page for an e-commerce team.

The catalog is read-heavy and should feel fast for day-to-day browsing, so avoid re-querying product data on every request.

Admins can trigger a "Sync latest catalog" action from the page when upstream ERP/PIM data changes (pricing, inventory, availability). After submitting, they should be able to continue working immediately, even if the product list is briefly stale.

The expected behavior is that product data is refreshed in the background and becomes up to date shortly after, across any views that depend on the same catalog data.

Implement the sync trigger as a regular HTML form that uses an inline Server Action in the page.
Use the cache tag name "products" consistently for catalog caching and invalidation.

In practice, sync jobs can touch thousands of SKUs, so operators prioritize a responsive admin experience and eventual consistency across catalog views over forcing every request to block on freshly recomputed data.

text: The app is extremely small right now, so I need one more pass through the exact cache-components requirements before editing. I’m checking whether `use cache` needs an explicit config flag in this installed Next version, then I’ll build the page and sync action around that.

The installed docs show `revalidateTag(tag, 'max')` is the intended stale-while-revalidate path for content like product catalogs, and `cacheComponents: true` is the Next 16 opt-in for `use cache`. I’m moving to code edits now: add tagged cached reads, a simulated sync mutation, and an inline form action that invalidates `products` without forcing the page to block.

text:
Implemented the admin catalog page with tagged cache reads and a regular HTML form using an inline Server Action.

The page now reads catalog data through a cached function in [app/page.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/page.tsx:30) using `'use cache'`, `cacheLife('hours')`, and `cacheTag('products')`. The sync button is a plain `<form action={syncLatestCatalog}>` in the same file, and the inline Server Action calls `revalidateTag('products', 'max')` after enqueueing the sync so the UI stays responsive and catalog views sharing the `products` tag refresh via stale-while-revalidate instead of blocking.

The simulated catalog source in [lib/db.js](/home/user/workspace/lib/db.js:1) now keeps revisioned product state plus a queued sync payload, so a sync request is lightweight and the refreshed catalog is applied on the next recomputation. I also enabled cache components in [next.config.ts](/home/user/workspace/next.config.ts:1), which this installed Next.js 16 build requires for `use cache`. Next updated [tsconfig.json](/home/user/workspace/tsconfig.json:31) during the build to add `.next/dev/types/**/*.ts`.

Verification: `npm run build` passed.
